report_name: Vercel Breach: Supply Chain Attack via AI Tool Exposes Customer Credentials

attack_patterns.name: Trusted Relationship
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor exploited the trusted relationship between Vercel and its third-party service provider, Context.ai, to gain initial access.

attack_patterns.name: Steal Application Access Token
attack_patterns.description: The attacker used a malicious OAuth application to steal an application access token, which enabled them to hijack a legitimate user session.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor hijacked a legitimate Vercel employee's Google Workspace account to perform lateral movement into Vercel's internal systems.

attack_patterns.name: Multi-Factor Authentication
attack_patterns.description: The attacker abused a compromised OAuth application's permissions to hijack a session, a technique that can bypass MFA and other traditional authentication controls.

attack_patterns.name: Credentials In Files
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor targeted and accessed customer credentials and secrets stored in non-sensitive environment variables on the Vercel platform.

tools.name: Context.ai
tools.description: A third-party AI tool used by a Vercel employee that was compromised by a threat actor and used as the entry point for a supply chain attack against Vercel.

campaigns.name: Vercel Supply Chain Attack via Context.ai
campaigns.description: A sophisticated supply chain attack where a threat actor compromised the third-party AI tool Context.ai to pivot into Vercel's internal systems and access customer environment variables.
campaigns.objective: Access customer data stored within environment variables on the Vercel platform.

identities.name: Vercel
identities.description: A web infrastructure provider that suffered a security breach originating from a compromised third-party AI tool, leading to the exposure of some customer environment variables.

identities.name: Context.ai
identities.description: A third-party AI tool provider whose Google Workspace OAuth application was compromised, serving as the initial vector for the supply chain attack against Vercel.

infrastructure.name: Malicious Google Workspace OAuth Application
infrastructure.description: A compromised Google Workspace OAuth application associated with Context.ai, identified by client ID 110671459871-30f1spbu0hptbs60cb4vsmv79i7bbvqj.apps.googleusercontent.com, used to hijack a user session and gain initial access.

indicators.name: Malicious Google Workspace OAuth App Detection
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Malicious Google Workspace OAuth App DNS Query
id: 2b1c8e9f-4d5a-4b6c-8f9a-1d2e3f4a5b6c
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries for a Google Workspace OAuth Application Client ID associated with the Vercel breach supply chain attack in April 2026.
references:
  - https://vercel.com/knowledge/security-incident-2026-04-19
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-19
tags:
  - attack.t1199
  - attack.initial_access
level: high
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName: '110671459871-30f1spbu0hptbs60cb4vsmv79i7bbvqj.apps.googleusercontent.com'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects activity related to the malicious Google Workspace OAuth Application Client ID used in the Vercel supply chain attack.
